opp.name: OmniaS2C24

We are reasonably close to finalisation and are currently closing out the internal alignment within the business. I will revert shortly with our consolidated position. opp.ais_why:
• Product scope is iContract, iSource, iSupplier and ML iAnalyze ‚Äî one ML module (ML iAnalyze for spend analytics) in scope but no Merlin, no Agentic AI, no ANA. The opp is in Contract In Progress stage so scope is already defined around analytics, not AI agents. The single ML module in a CLM/Sourcing scope is a thin AI signal.
• Contract In Progress stage with 10 valid Avoma meetings on the Omnia account including 7 Apr 2026 ~81min completed with Ragan Webb and Zama Khambula, 18 Mar 2026 ~60min with Junaid Sulaiman, Tracy Seeley-Jeppe and Marianne Ventura, and 19 Feb 2026 ~2hr session with 24 Omnia attendees including Kevin Browne and Dieter Sparbier. Heavy late-stage engagement.
• BD_Engagement empty; no agentic platform discussion captured in allowed business-text fields and no AI champion narrative in Description or meeting notes; the advanced contracting stage suggests scope is locked around analytics not AI transformation. Boundary defaulted DOWN at 4/5. Medium confidence based on valid meeting evidence and clear product scope (ML iAnalyze + traditional CLM/sourcing).

sources: Avoma SOW closure call 30 Jun 2026 (Marianne on manual screening and 3-week spend lag)
Avoma SOW review 23 Jun 2026 (6,000-contract / 6,000-supplier migration scope)
SF Products__c (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier)
metrics.narrative: Omnia is digitizing end-to-end Source-to-Contract across roughly USD 1bn of managed spend (raised from USD 900m on the 30 Jun call) spanning multiple business units in South Africa, the rest of Africa, Brazil, Australia and China. The operational pain is concrete and buyer-stated: Marianne Ventura on 30 Jun described supplier screening done manually in Worldcheck One ('as we're having to do it manually now, it's one of the things that's quite painful for me') and spend data arriving three weeks after month-close ('I can't wait 3 weeks to get that'). Migration scope is quantified in the SOW at 6,000 legacy contracts and 6,000 suppliers. The value case is a clear qualitative and volumetric one (manual-to-automated screening, single contract repository, faster spend analytics via ML iAnalyze); a formal ROI/savings number is not signed, so quantifying dollar savings remains the one open metric.

sources: Avoma SOW closure 30 Jun 2026 (Marianne closing tone and ownership)
Avoma SOW review 23 Jun 2026 (Dieter engaged on integration)
SF LastActivityDate 2026-07-01
champion.narrative: Marianne Ventura (Senior Category Manager: IT & Communications) is a strong, active champion who has driven the deal end-to-end and is visibly emotionally invested at the finish ('I never want to do another one of these by email ever again... it's incredibly exciting'). She has access to the economic buyers, owns the operational decision, controls the internal legal-cover routing, and proactively pulls Zycus toward close. A second, technically credible advocate emerged in Dieter Sparbier (Head of IT Architecture), who is engaged and constructive on integration. What is still missing is nothing structural: the only open champion dependency is Marianne completing Omnia's internal sign-off routing, which she has committed to. Engagement is continuous through the 01 Jul LastActivityDate; no cooling.

sources: SF Competitors__c (Coupa; iValua)
Prior record: 17 Oct 2024 discovery (Coupa/iValua elimination rationale)
Avoma 30 Jun 2026 (Letsema partner replacement, manual screening displacement)
competition.narrative: This is effectively a sole-source finish. Coupa and iValua (the two Salesforce-logged competitors) were both eliminated earlier in the cycle: Coupa on cost and complexity for the multi-entity Africa/APAC rollout and weaker native CLM, iValua on weaker contract lifecycle depth. The genuine 'competitor' now is Omnia's incumbent manual state (Excel/manual screening in Worldcheck One and Inoxico, 6,000 contracts scattered across SharePoint, Teams and file servers) which Zycus is displacing, plus Adobe Sign being replaced by Certinal for e-signature. There is no live multi-vendor fight in contracting; the only competitive-adjacent open item is Omnia replacing implementation partner Letsema, where Zycus is positioning to absorb change management itself. We win by making Merlin contract-discovery and native iContract the wedge that manual tools and bolt-on suites cannot match.

sources: Avoma SOW closure 30 Jun 2026 (manual screening, 3-week spend lag)
Avoma SOW review 23 Jun 2026 (Marianne on 2020 cyber incident and missing supplier-master fields)
identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain, in Marianne Ventura's own words on 30 Jun, is manual supplier screening in Worldcheck One and Inoxico ('it's one of the things that's quite painful for me... we spend a lot of time filtering that out') and slow spend visibility ('after a month closes, it's 3 weeks later... I can't wait 3 weeks'). A structural driver is a 2020 cyber incident that left gaps in supplier-master data, which Omnia wants bridged by extracting fields from contracts via the relevant stakeholder pain owner is Marianne (procurement/category), with Dieter Sparbier owning the integration-standard pain. This is confirmed operational and data-governance pain, not inferred.

sources: Avoma SOW closure 30 Jun 2026 (signatories Caroline / Tejal Sawant, Adobe Sign, Certinal)
SF task 2026-06-30 (Validation of Implementation Partner Scope - Letsema Replacement)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Tracy Seeley-Jeppe (Head of Legal, Risk, and Compliance)
paper_process.narrative: Three documents are in flight: the MSA, Order 1 and the SOW. On 30 Jun the MSA comments were cleaned (Zycus legal head Zoheb) and Order 1 aligned to the USD 1bn spend figure; Caroline confirmed she signs the MSA and Order 1 for Zycus, while the SOW is signed by the relevant stakeholder, AVP Global Delivery. Omnia's paper mechanics require an internal legal cover and an internal Adobe Sign audit trail before the documents come out for external signature via Certinal (chosen partly because it is enforceable in China). Tracy Seeley-Jeppe (Head of Legal, Risk, and Compliance) owns Omnia legal. The remaining friction is the SOW scope increase, and a separate paper thread is Omnia's plan to replace Letsema as implementation partner (Marianne asked Zycus to validate partner scope on 30 Jun). No contract-expiration forcing function; the forcing function is Omnia's own stated need to 'report we're ready to sign' within days.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Stephan Serfontein (Group Financial Director), Jacques De Villiers (Group: Head of Supply Chain)
Avoma 30 Jun 2026 (scope rise to USD 1bn, price held flat)
Prior record: 19 Feb 2026 SOW review commercial approval
economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with Stephan Serfontein (Group Financial Director) and Jacques De Villiers (Group: Head of Supply Chain), both Salesforce contact roles, with the USD 234k commercial approved during the 19 Feb 2026 SOW review. At this contracting stage the commercial envelope is settled: on 30 Jun Zycus confirmed the USD 900m-to-USD 1bn scope rise attracts no additional charge, removing the last budget question. The EBs are currently passive (neither appears on the 17 Jun, 23 Jun or 30 Jun calls, which Marianne and legal drove), which is normal for a late-stage legal close; the live commercial-sign figure is instead being carried by Marianne and Tracy's legal cover. No budget risk is evident.

sources: Avoma SOW closure 30 Jun 2026 (Marianne on internal legal cover -> Adobe Sign -> hand to Zycus)
Prior record: 17 Jun 2026 legal alignment (design-doc enforceability)
SF Next_Step__c 08 Apr 2026 (Junaid escalation)
decision_process.narrative: The process is read directly off the call sequence: design-document enforceability was resolved on the 17 Jun legal alignment call, the SOW was reviewed line-by-line with Dieter Sparbier on 23 Jun, and every open SOW comment was closed on the 30 Jun call ('all the comments are resolved... the SOW is ready'). The approval chain Marianne described on 30 Jun is: Omnia does one final internal run-through, drafts the SOW into their legal cover, routes it through an internal Adobe Sign audit trail (legal cover not shared with Zycus), then hands it to Zycus for signature. Junaid Sulaiman is Marianne's manager and the named escalation path (08 Apr Next Step). This is a well-defined, buyer-run contracting process, not a picklist.

sources: Avoma SOW review 23 Jun 2026 (Dieter on integration standards, Merlin metadata extraction, mastering)
Avoma SOW closure 30 Jun 2026 (clause version control, Certinal, spend upload)
SF Next_Step__c (Certinal e-sign)
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation has moved from selection criteria to execution criteria, all confirmed on recent calls: native contract lifecycle depth (iContract authoring, redlining, version control on clauses), Merlin AI metadata extraction to auto-populate fields on 6,000 legacy contracts, single source-of-truth mastering with D365 as transactional master and Zycus as supplier/contract master, and integration that complies with Omnia's own event-based OIB (integration bus) standards. Dieter Sparbier set a hard criterion on 23 Jun that integrations must conform to Omnia's open-API framework ('that's not negotiable... we need to comply to that'). Certinal was confirmed as the e-signature standard because it is workable across Omnia's geographies including China. These are confirmed, buyer-owned criteria captured in the SOW and design documents.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Contract In Progress' anchors near 85. Win signals and momentum lift the underlying read to ~104 — but we cap confidence at 100 until the stage advances and sign-off lands. Read the gap as 'earned, but not yet banked.'

cro_panel.header: Omnia Holdings Limited — Contract In Progress · $234K · closes 31 Jul

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 99 is carried by preference strength +1.00 (weight 20); champion strength +1.00 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): Next Step carries 9 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 25 reflects real customer investment: Direct economic-buyer access recorded.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 96 is anchored on the stage win (99), nudged by commitment 25 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

reasons.detail: On 2 Jul the close date of this USD 234k forecasted Commit deal was moved from 30 Jun to 31 Jul, a full month slip out of the June quarter. Engagement is otherwise strong (last activity 2 days ago, MSA and Order 1 out for signature), so this is a paperwork-timing slip on Omnia's internal legal cover and an increased-scope SOW, not disengagement. If ignored, the risk is a forecasted commit rolling again should the Adobe Sign audit trail and legal routing stall past the 15 Jul buyer target.
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Caroline exactly what slipped the Omnia close from Jun 30 to Jul 31 and whether signed MSA and Order 1 will land by the 15 Jul buyer target.
reasons.summary: Forecast Commit deal Omnia pushed close date Jun 30 to Jul 31
reasons.evidence: recent_movements_14d: CloseDate old 2026-06-30 -> new 2026-07-31, changed by Rashi Varma on 2026-07-02; next_step 'June 30 2026: all docs out for signature'.

critical_signals.text: On 30 June every open SOW comment was resolved and Marianne declared the statement of work ready to return for signature.
Managed spend rose from about 900 million to a billion dollars yet Zycus held the price flat, removing the last commercial objection.
This is a sole-source finish: Coupa and iValua were eliminated earlier and no rival is active in contracting.
Omnia is replacing implementation partner Letsema and asked Zycus to validate partner scope, an opening to own change management directly.
Dieter Sparbier, Head of IT Architecture, is now actively engaged and set a non-negotiable integration-standard the design must meet.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and warming toward signature: dense email cadence through 01 Jul, buyer-stated urgency to 'report we're ready to sign,' and all SOW comments resolved. No cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: 28 days to close (close 2026-07-31). Deal created 22 May 2024, 774 days old. Stage path Qualified -> Formal Evaluation -> Shortlisted -> Vendor Selected -> Negotiation -> Contract In Progress. Close date slipped four times in 2026 (10 Apr -> 30 Apr -> 29 May -> 30 Jun -> 31 Jul, latest push 02 Jul by Rashi Varma). LastActivityDate 01 Jul (2 days ago) confirms live execution. Remaining path is Omnia internal legal cover + Adobe Sign audit trail, then countersignature by Zycus (Tejal Sawant on SOW, Caroline on MSA/Order 1). No new evaluation or commercial negotiation left; pace is recoverable but the 31 Jul date is tight given internal legal routing.
evidence: Ground truth: stage Contract In Progress, forecast Commit, close 2026-07-31, LastActivityDate 2026-07-01

30 Jun 2026 SOW closure call: all comments resolved, Marianne 'the SOW is ready... I look forward to getting it back' and 'we've gotta go in a couple of days, and we have to report we're ready to sign'
30 Jun 2026: managed-spend scope raised from USD 900m to USD 1bn, Zycus held price flat ('we won't increase any commercials')
Field history 02 Jul 2026: CloseDate 2026-06-30 -> 2026-07-31 (by Rashi Varma)
26 Jun 2026 Next Step: Order 1 and MSA sent for signing, SOW still needs extra work due to scope increase
Engagement pulse: LIVE, LastActivityDate 2026-07-01, active email + Avoma cadence through 01 Jul
north_star_verdict.headline: Won deal in final signature: all SOW comments resolved 30 Jun and MSA/Order 1/SOW out for signing, but close date already pushed to 31 Jul so timing, not the deal, is the only risk.

flags: Lock a mutual signing timeline with Marianne: internal legal cover, Adobe Sign audit trail, then Zycus countersignature (Tejal on SOW, Caroline on MSA/Order 1) - close date already slipped to 31 Jul.
Close the integration thread with Dieter Sparbier by exchanging Swagger specs and Omnia's OIB framework before design workshops, so event-based conformance is not a post-signature surprise.
Convert the USD 1bn scope rise and Letsema partner-replacement into a written change-management proposal that keeps Zycus (not an external SI) owning implementation.
best_practice_check.summary: Late-stage win in final signature; protect the 31 Jul close by de-risking internal legal routing and the two open technical threads.

customer_preference.evidence: Marianne on 30 Jun: 'I never want to do another one of these by email ever again... it's incredibly exciting'; chose Zycus native change management over an external partner.

competitive_position.summary: No active competitive threat in contracting: this is a sole-source finish. Coupa (eliminated on cost/complexity, 2024) and iValua (eliminated on weaker CLM, 2024) are both out; the displaced incumbent is Omnia's manual Excel/Worldcheck process and scattered file-store contracts; Adobe Sign is being replaced by Certinal. The only competitive-adjacent live item is Omnia replacing implementation partner Letsema, where Zycus is positioning to take change management in-house. Strongest current 'threat' is execution risk (SOW scope, internal legal routing), not a rival.
